findings.rule: Prefer direct use of the existing policy owner when a wrapper does not adapt semantics or remove caller knowledge.

evidence.detail: AccessPolicyAdapter forwards the same actor and resource directly to is_allowed and has one caller.
Nearby code calls policy functions directly when no semantic adaptation exists.

findings.concern: The adapter moves one call behind a class without removing any branch, parameter, or policy concept.
findings.impact: It adds a public concept, allocation, method, and call-path hop while leaving the caller's knowledge unchanged.
findings.proposed_change: Remove AccessPolicyAdapter and return is_allowed(actor, resource) directly from can_view.
findings.expected_effect: Remove one class, one method, one allocation, and one call hop without changing policy behavior.

next_action: Remove the pass-through adapter and retain direct use of is_allowed.
